steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined portion. Read it carefully: “To conclude that this approach accounts for the ethereal actualities now synonymous with the Highwaymen aesthetic is tempting but inaccurate.” The phrase “tempting but inaccurate” signals that the sentence raises an interpretation only to reject it. The rest of the passage then provides the reason it’s inaccurate: Hair’s methods weren’t universally practiced.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It explains how an artist mentioned earlier in the text developed a distinctive style by adapting a particular approach to painting originated by his colleagues.: The underlined portion is not about how any single artist developed a style — it’s about rejecting a group-level interpretation. ✗
  • Choice B — It considers and rebuts an interpretation of the effect of a painting technique mentioned earlier in the text on the perception of work by a group of artists.: Precisely what the underlined portion does — it floats the interpretation that Hair’s fast painting created the Highwaymen aesthetic, then rejects it. ✓
  • Choice C — It establishes a contrast between the aesthetic qualities of works by artists who were central to a movement introduced earlier in the text and those of an artist who was more peripheral to that movement.: No aesthetic contrast is drawn; the point is that the same aesthetic was achieved by different methods. ✗
  • Choice D — It details evidence that contradicts a claim mentioned earlier in the text about a long-standing disagreement within a group of artists.: No prior disagreement was mentioned in the text. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
